title: Container for hydrogen peroxide solutions
abstract: This invention relates to a container for storing an aqueous hydrogen peroxide solution, the container comprising: walls formed from a gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material, the walls being capable of permitting gas to escape from the container through the walls to avoid build-up of pressure within the container while retaining liquid within the container, the polymeric material having a gas transmission rate in the range from about 0.05 to about 0.5 cubic centimeters of gas per square centimeter of polymeric material per 24 hours at 25Â°C.
claims: Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. A container for storing an aqueous hydrogen peroxide solution, the container comprising: walls formed from a gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material, the walls being capable of permitting gas to escape from the container through the walls to avoid build-up of pressure within the container while retaining liquid within the container, the polymeric material having a gas transmission rate in the range from about 0.05 to about 0.5 cubic centimeters of gas per square centimeter of polymeric material per 24 hours at 25Â°C. 2. The container of claim 1, wherein the container comprises two or more walls joined together along opposing edges to define a fluid reservoir. 3. The container of claim 1 wherein the container comprises a cylindrical construction. 4. The container of any of the preceding claims, wherein the gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material comprises a polyolefin, polyester, polyamide, vinyl chloride polymer, polyacrylate, polycarbonate, or a mixture of two or more thereof. 5. The container of any of the preceding claims, wherein the gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material comprises polyethylene, polypropylene, polyethylene terephthalate, copolymers of polyethylene terephthalate, polybutylene terephthalate, polyvinyl chloride, polyvinylidene fluoride, ethylene vinyl alcohol, ethylene vinyl acetate, polychoroprene, polyisoprene, polytetrafluoroethylene, poly (trifluorochloroethylene), poly methacrylate, poly (meth) methacrylate, or a mixture of two or more thereof. 6. The container of any of the preceding claims, wherein the gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material comprises a vinyl film. 7. The container of any of the preceding claims, wherein the walls have a thickness in the range of about 25 Î¼Î¹Î· to about 250 Î¼Î¹Î·. 8. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material comprises a vinyl film with a gas transmission rate in the range from about 0.1 to about 0.3 cubic centimeters of gas per square centimeter of film material per 24 hours at 25Â°C. 9. The container of claim 7 wherein the gas transmission rate is in the range from about 0.18 to about 0.26 cubic centimeters of gas per square centimeter of film material per 24 hours at 25Â°C. 10. The container of any of the preceding claims, wherein the container further includes an opening for allowing an aqueous hydrogen peroxide solution to be added to the container or to be removed from the container. 11. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the container has an internal volume in the range from about 0.05 to about 30 liters. 12. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein an aqueous hydrogen peroxide solution is present in the container. 13. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is from about 3% by about 70% by weight. 14. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is from about 3% by about 6% by weight. 15. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is from about 10% by about 70% by weight. 16. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is from about 30% by about 40% by weight. 17. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is about 35% by weight. 18. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is from about 55 to about 65% by weight. 19. The container of claim 12 wherein the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the aqueous solution is about 59% by weight. 20. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the container is in the form of a bag, pouch or bottle. 21. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the container comprises an IV bag. 22. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the container is characterized by the absence of a vent for allowing passage of gas out of the container. 23. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein at least about 50% of the surface area of the walls of the container is formed from the gas permeable, liquid impermeable polymeric material. 24. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the internal volume of the container is no more than about 25% more than the volume of the aqueous hydrogen peroxide solution to be stored in the container. 25. The container of any of the preceding claims wherein the walls are formed from a mono-layered polymeric material. 26. The container of any of claims 1-24 wherein the walls are formed from a multi-layered polymeric material.
